summary.intent: Fix repeated 'address already in use' errors when the Gossamer VS Code extension starts its server, and resolve 'no tables found' errors causing app startup failure.
summary.outcome: Fixed three stacked bugs: orphaned server process handling in GossamerPanel, silent no-op in pushSchema due to comment-line filtering, and corrupt SQLite DB recovery with cache eviction and retry logic.

repo: GossamerPanel.server is a static in-memory variable; it resets on VS Code reload/crash, leaving orphaned server processes holding the port.
pushSchema runs on the primary DB (CLI arg) at startup but did NOT run on secondary repo DBs already in config — only on newly registered repos via POST /api/repos.
getDb uses a module-level dbCache keyed by path; a broken Prisma client is cached and persists even if the file is deleted/recreated within the same process.
The gossamer server manages multiple repo DBs: one primary (passed via CLI), and N secondary repos from config, each with their own .db file.
schema.sql is bundled into dist/schema.sql and read at runtime by pushSchema.

code.finding: pushSchema(db) here only covers the primary DB; secondary repo DBs were never receiving schema at startup.
httpServer.listen(port) had no EADDRINUSE error handler, causing silent crashes on port conflict.

pushSchema split schema.sql on ';\n' then filtered chunks with !s.startsWith('--'). Every chunk in schema.sql starts with a -- CreateTable comment, so all 56 statements were silently discarded — DBs were created but had zero tables.
Added evictDb() export to allow callers to remove a cached Prisma client by path, enabling retry logic after corrupt DB deletion.
spawnServer() guard only checked in-memory GossamerPanel.server; did not account for orphaned processes from previous sessions still holding the port.

workflow: macOS com.apple.provenance extended attribute on SQLite files can cause 'disk I/O error' — SQLite cannot open quarantined/provenance-flagged DB files.
When debugging silent failures in SQL schema application, always verify the actual statement count after filtering — zero statements with no error is a common failure mode.
Testing pushSchema in isolation (node --input-type=module) can pass even when the compiled dist path fails; always verify with the actual compiled output.
For port-conflict recovery, killing via lsof + SIGTERM + 500ms wait is more reliable than trying to adopt or reuse an existing server process.

friction: Multiple failed iterations before root cause was found: first 'adopt orphan' approach caused stale server reuse; then the schema fix compiled correctly but still failed due to a second stacked bug (disk I/O error on gossamer-test.db).
The comment-filter bug in pushSchema caused completely silent failure — DBs were created, 'schema ready' was logged, but zero tables existed. No error was surfaced.
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr blocking SQLite I/O was unexpected and hard to diagnose — appeared as a generic 'disk I/O error' with no indication of the xattr cause.
getDb cache meant deleting a corrupt DB file was insufficient — the broken Prisma client remained cached and would be reused, requiring explicit cache eviction.
open_items: The restart() method in GossamerPanel also needs to wait for the old server to fully die before spawning a new one — acknowledged but not fully addressed.
No HTTP shutdown endpoint on the server — killing orphaned processes requires shelling out to lsof, which is fragile and platform-specific (macOS only).
The worktree conflict error ('entire/checkpoints/v1 is already used by worktree') for gossamer-test was logged but not addressed.
The disk I/O / xattr issue was fixed reactively (delete + retry); there is no proactive check or warning when a DB file has problematic extended attributes.
